cross_aspect_duplicates.sentence: first, the tv's 500 channels could afford to have one or two more english channels than cnn and bbc
the bathroom was big, with plenty of fluffy towels and the bedroom had a large tv with a good selection of channels

the first impression when i arrived it was a cosy entrance and friendly staff (well done manuel) the room was ok for single use (comfortable bed and pillows - clean - mini bar - big flat tv) and it was

modern items like tv, mini bar and safe are built into antique furnishings; towelling robes and slippers are free; and the shower had a rather sinister looking handheld device for "accurate rinsing :-)"
as a minus point i would say the breakfast could be better and maybe a spa/pool to better justify the price (the hotel doesn't come cheap compared to other 4 star hotels in rome )
there's no pool or spa -- i sort of expect a pool for a four star hotel
the room itself had a retro 70's feel with a comfortable living room and kitchen area, a separate bedroom with a nice king size bed, and a sink area outside the shower/toilet area
the little work out room is kind of a joke, but it is a clean and well appointed joke...the tv (w/headphones) on the exercise machines is nice
room with 2 x double beds, shower over (tiny) bath, toilet, sink in room, hair dryer, tv, iron and board, sitting table and 2 x chairs
we got a ground floor room which had tv with cable(bbc world news was nice for us brits), huge bed, free wi fi, coffee maker but no fridge or microwave
the tv in the bedroom was situated high in the corner of the bedroom on the type of platform you would find in a hospital room
there was a safe and tv
i had a huge bed, flat screen tv, comfortable chairs, coffee maker (and real cream is provided!)

* small rooms * we had a small balcony but had plastic furniture and latice on the floor (not nice) * the dresser, desk and minibar are still the old furniture (too many pieces and old and ugly) it did

coffee shop open 24hrs where you can get just about anything to eat such as bagels, donuts, pizza, ice cream, sandwiches, drinks,etc.good accomodations in the room but could use one more lamp and a comfortable lounging chair
only thing wrong was no tea or coffee making facilities, but they had satellite tv
the next room next door was listening to the tv very loud
another thing is that if you want to watch tv and don't speak italian you'll have to pay extra to get additional channels in english and movies etc
the bathroom was very clean and modern (i can't stand fusty old bathrooms), the bed was comfy, you get a desk with wireless access and tv, microwave and fridge
small kitchenette with stove and fridge (no microwave), large dinning area, living area (comfortable couch and chair - a small tv, but wasnt in the room much, so that was fine.)
the biggest flat screen tv ever and the staff's attentiveness was superior: when i set the ac temp too low and froze the coils, within moments they sent up a megawatt air purifier/fan

the bedroom was fine, usual excellent bedding and linens and the bathroom lovely and spacious with a great rainhead shower; yes, the tv in the bathroom mirror is a gimmick, but we found it entertaining (never seen that before)
the room was really nice and the tv had more than 12 stations
the rooms were nicely decorated, a little noisy, but we ran the fan so we wouldn't hear the neighbors tv
drinks taken in the hotel bar are a pleasure, with friendly bar staff that can mix cocktails and long drinks with skill and a smile; nothing is too much for them

our room had a kitchenette which was convenient, but since the spa is located only steps away from a variety of restaurants (high, medium and low end), we just used it for the refrigerator and early morning coffee
you can purchase a pass for a great fitness center with steam room and lap pool
the pool, gym and hot tub on the top floor was fantastic, with a view of the city

the pool was nice as was the fitness center but with tiny cramped rooms, lack of customer service (i tried calling the desk 3 times and never go through) and the loading dock at the front of the building, i
we had a gorgeous, spacious living room with a fireplace and a big bathroom with a double size jacuzzi tub that opened into the bedroom

a few minor annoyances for us were: 1) hot water in the shower wasn't nearly hot enough, although the tub hot water was; 2) only a table top (not full size) size ironing board was provided (delivered promptly upon request)
the roof top pool and lounge area with bar was our favorite place to meet for a drink and the views of the city at the end of the day
do not expect a pool or spa
the room was very spacious with a separate sitting area with its own dedicated tv in addition to the 24" lcd adjacent to the queen size bed
the staff at the hotel and the parking company (ace parking) are working hard to rectify the situation, but at $27/night for parking one would hope that her car was more secure than it apparently was
hotel was ok and in a very good location, but the â‚¬45 per night parking charge was extortionate
last thing, bring a travel iron, the hotel room will not provide you with an iron, they charge 15euro for an ironing service, and you must give the item in bye 10am that day
there is a small fitness center and laundry in the basement
the service by all the employees from bell staff, bar, front desk, spa, concierge desk, housekeeping, all of them are perfect professionals who aims to please all their customers
in room facilities are great like the free safe, wifi and hair dryer all good touches
also, free wifi in the lobby and/or rooms would have been nice but the free use of a computer in the lobby was convenient
highly recommended and i will have no hesitation in staying there on future visits to montreal - way better than one of those 80s / 90s chain hotels that claim to be 4 or 5 star
due to a lack of avilability our standard rooms, we were upgraded to a junior suite for the same price we paid for the standard room
the bathrooms were brand new and large (no bath, just shower) but the rooms were compact
all in all pretty good value for money, but don’t expect the rooms to resemble luxury spa accommodation – they are closer to motel standard
the staff were friendly and helpful, and the lobby and bars downstairs were very relaxing and we did spend alot of time there
the location is perfect - really close to the main action and yet situated on a peaceful side canal so you get a good nights' sleep
i would highly recommend the doubletree and plan on staying here again on our next trip
we will definitely stay here again on our next trip to chicago
i would definitely stay at this hotel again
overall, a great stay and i would definitely stay there again, especially in the summer when there are tons of nearby patios to explote
we would stay here again, if you want more luxury it is not for you, if you want a clean safe place and conveniently located for parks then its perfect
we had decided that we wanted to stay an extra night in puerto rico to visit more of the island after we completed our carribean cruise
if we ever return to rome, which if our wish when throwing coins in the trevi fountain ensures we will, we will definitely stay at the una hotal
we enjoyed our stay and would highly recommend the hotel in this fantastic city
i was pleased with my stay overall and would have no reservations in staying again on my next trip
i have stayed at this hotel both times i visited paris, and if i get the opportunity to return, i would stay there again
i would stay here again but be a little more carefull about expensive specials in future
this was a great place to stay while visiting rome, we would highly recomend it
iwould strongly recommend the grand hotel to everyone i know and any and all who ask me where to stay while in ocean city
made our stay the best of venice, rome and florence, after a good stay in florence and a disapointing stay at the de russie in rome
would definitely stay here again, however, we may want to stay in a different area if we go back
we would stay here again on a return trip to venice
we've been to chicago several times and i think this hotel stay was my favorite
i am glad i did because i absolutely enjoyed my stay and would definitely stay there again
on our experience, we would be happy to stay here again
if we ever need to stay in a hotel in seattle again,we would definitely stay at the four seasons
i would recommend a stay at the erwin (for great access to venice beach), they do know how to look after you
i understand that she feel irritated maybe because a lot of people is calling them and asking them what to do, but still, i am the customer, and i only want my safety
the room was spacious, clean, and had all the modern amenities...stove, sink, separate living room and bedroom (each with a television), large tiled shower and sepate bath
since they are in the midst of building a massive building across the street, it was fairly noisy but once we closed the windows, the noise was muted
an unexpected bonus was the room layout: one bedroom, then the bathroom area, then another bedroom with a door that closed off the rest of the hotel room
the hotel has a modern design (no this is not the olympia) with a fireplace going non-stop in the lobby, lovely marble bathrooms and the use of light coloured wood in rooms
the room was clean but worn the ac/heat was very noisy and the towels really needed to be replaced
the air conditioning was excellent, and while the wi-fi connection was inconsistent at times (they turn the wi-fi off at 8am on the day of checkout), this wasn't a major issue
the decor was quaint, the rooms small (we expected that), beds were very comfortable and the air conditioning worked
bathroom well equiped - big shower and big sink, you weren't balancing your stuff on top of things you would rather not touch
i originally tried to book a room in a hotel near the san marco plaza, but believe me you will love the quiet neighborhood, away from all the throngs and tourist action
the only complaint we really had was there were no plugs next to the bedside tables so our cell phones (also double as alarm clocks) were on the other side of the room
i highly recommend this hotel to everyone and i would definitely stay there if i ever go to amsterdam again
the staff are very friendly and helpful all speak english and french and are helpful with dinner reservations
the staff were friendly, helpful and spoke excellent english
the staff were friendly, spoke perfect english, and there was no effort spared to make our stay enjoyable and relaxing
